review_title: Drive that just works, but folders get messy with small sync bugs
description: Overall I use Drive for almost everything, work docs, notes, and quick sharing. It makes teamwork easier because everyone sees latest file and no more email attachments. Small sync hiccups and messy folders are annoying but not dealbreakers for me. The free tier is handy for personal use and company plan gives enough storage. I’d recommend it to most teams, just set folder rules so people don’t dump files everywhere. For daily work it saves time and keeps things simple, that’s why I keep using it.
author: Harshul S.
pros: I use Google Drive every day at work and it just opens fast, no waiting. Sharing files is super simple, I just copy link and people get access, no fuss. Collaboration in Docs and Sheets is great, we edit same file at same time and no conflicts. Search finds old files even when I forget the name, that saves me lots of time. Auto save has saved me more than once when my laptop crashed mid edit. Integrations with Gmail and calendar make life easier, everything just links up.
cons: One thing I am not fond of is that it is connected to email storage , i have drive on my phone and it has automatic back up on and its almost filled up my 15 gb storage and since I use smae email it is creating problems in a email storage as well Folders get messy fast when everyone saves in their own way, hard to keep tidy. Offline sync sometimes fails and I had to reupload files after a trip. Big files upload slow on bad wifi and it can be annoying waiting. Permissions can be confusing, I once shared a folder too open by mistake. Version history is useful but finding the exact old version is not always easy. Not great for heavy media management, needs a proper DAM for big teams.
author_profession: Senior. Technical Support Specialist for Network Operations

review_title: The Future of Collaborative Online Work
description: At the end of the day, the constant updates that Google Drive does to maintain its relevance in the workspace are more beneficial that harmful. It keeps me up to date with technological advancements because it so clearly outlines changes that are being made to the interface. As a user who prioritizes understanding a quickly growing world, this is increasingly important. Google continues to innovate and adopt popular functions, so I never feel left behind.
author: Rebecca M.
pros: Google Drive is invaluable in so many aspects of my day-to-day life. It allows me to easily transition between work and personal life all within the same platform. I can organize and share files when needed, save documents to be accessed online from any computer, and manage any of Google's primary products in one convenient space.
cons: The recent integration of AI into Google products with Gemini has felt altogether unnatural and early. I can appreciate some aspects of the assistance that artificial intelligence provides, but I've had issues recently that are entirely due to the inclusion of this new feature.

review_title: Google Drives me impatient
author: Jesse C.
pros: Essentiel workspace ecosystem. Easy to use interface. Overall handy product with a fast loading time.
cons: The main bad aspect is the search bar, which is also a highly important one -- the most important and critical. It is difficult to find the documents we need.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: The gold standard for collaboration but AI is making it bloated.
description: Overall, Google Drive is still the Gold standard for collaborative working and writing. However, the AI has made the software bloated and difficult to navigate. Every task now has an AI popup and there's no way to turn them off.
author: Abbey W.
pros: Every writer I know uses Google Drive as a collaborative tool. It's great for editing documents together and you don't have the problem of "version control." I love that you can see the revision history so when changes are made you can understand who made them and why. It's so intuitive to use and the cost is negligible.
cons: Organizing files in Google Drive has always been somewhat of a nightmare and the AI components make it so much worse. I can't find what I'm looking for and I spend too much time just telling Google that I do not want to use AI for basic tasks. The Gemini features are just making basic use so bloated and difficult to use.

review_title: Google Drive - I trust my business to this storage system
description: Google Drive is, hands down, the best online storage option. I have used most of the competing systems, including Onedrive, DropBox and others. None compare with regards to speed, cost or quality.
author: Brad A.
pros: I use Google Drive for all of my data storage. As a part of my subscription for emails, I get a huge amount of storage for the lowest cost anywhere (excellent value). Any issue I have had has been quickly resolved with their customer support options. It is the easiest system to use - far easier than other storage services. I literally store all of my documents on this system and trust it to be accessible for well over a decade. It is easy to share files with my clients and to control access to the files at the file level! It is simple to take notes and attach them to a particular project using Google Notes and Sheets.
cons: I literally cannot think of a single think I would improve with Google Drive. It just works. If I had to name a thing I like least, it is the AI ability to understand my pdf files.

review_title: Reliable cloud storage and collaboration hub for a growing property management business
description: Overall, Google Drive is a dependable, everyday tool that simplifies document management and collaboration for our property management business. It gives us confidence that critical files are backed up, easy to find, and simple to share with the people who need them, which is why I’m happy to recommend it to other small and mid-sized real estate or service businesses.
author: Kartikeya G.
pros: As a property manager at Minikaal Corporation, Google Drive has become the central hub for our leases, owner reports, inspection photos, and vendor documents across multiple properties. The ability to securely store everything in one place and access it from any device has reduced our dependence on local computers and helped keep our operations running smoothly even when we are on-site or traveling between units. The biggest pros for us are real-time collaboration, simple sharing, and integration with other Google tools. We regularly collaborate in Docs, Sheets, and Slides directly from Drive, which makes it easy to work with owners, tenants, and bookkeepers without sending endless email attachments. Sharing links with permission controls (view, comment, edit) is intuitive even for less tech-savvy clients, and the search function makes it fast to find the right lease or invoice when we are on a call. The storage is reasonably priced as our portfolio grows, and team/shared drives help us keep company files separate from personal ones.
cons: A couple of areas that could improve are storage management and offline use. As we add more photos, videos, and scanned documents, it is easy to run into storage limits and then spend time cleaning up or upgrading plans. Offline access works, but it can be a bit clunky to manage across multiple devices, and new team members sometimes need guidance on how Drive syncs to their computer.

review_title: All-Inclusive and Seamless for Good Work
description: I use it for everything, from my personal life to the various enterprises I am involved in. It helps me do what I need to in an organized and pleasant manner.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: User friendliness, clear interface and great AI suggestions. There are so many features that I know I will be able to do all my work in one place, and I can share whatever I need in the capacity I need with anyone easily. I can easily find my work and organize it clearly.
cons: It doesn't seamlessly work with other platforms, and often I need to download documents to my computer before uploading to Drive

review_title: Reliable Backbone for Document Management and Operational Workflows
description: Overall, Google Drive has been a core component of our digital infrastructure. It enables efficient collaboration, secure storage, and easy access to company data across multiple projects and teams. It works particularly well when combined with other tools to build automated workflows and document management systems. While it is not a full document management system on its own, it provides a very strong and reliable foundation for building scalable operational processes.
author: Valentin P.
pros: Google Drive is extremely reliable and essential for managing company data and documents in a structured way. It integrates seamlessly with tools like Google Sheets, Forms, and third-party platforms, making it ideal for operational workflows. We use it to store site documents, delivery notes, invoices, and automated uploads from forms, which creates a clear audit trail and centralised access. The ability to control permissions, share files easily, and access everything from any device is a major advantage.
cons: Managing large volumes of files and folders can become difficult without a well-defined structure. Search works well, but organisation still relies heavily on manual folder management. There is also limited native functionality for advanced document control, such as version enforcement or structured workflows, which sometimes requires external tools or internal processes.

review_title: The most convenient cloud storage available
description: Google Drive's convenience and pricing cannot be beat. Whether you're a professional desktop user or you use Gmail personally on your phone, Drive is an essential tool for handling and backing up your documents.
author: Andrew Z.
pros: Google Drive is easily one of the cheapest and most convenient cloud storage options available. If you are a Gmail user, Drive cannot be any easier to use whether it be on mobile or desktop. Google's integration with Docs and Sheets makes editing documents and spreadsheets incredibly easy too. Drive is used daily with my company to collaborate and share important forms between departments.
cons: Google is not shy about about lack of privacy with Gmail and drive. It isn't the most secure option. Their customer support won't even touch the subject. If security and privacy are your highest priority, you'd be better off created your own NAS storage.

review_title: One stop destination for Cloud Storage Solution
description: Have been using google drive to store photos/videos for my side-hustle photography/videography business.
author: Swagat G.
pros: Very easy to use, fast, and smooth to share files to people. What I absolutely love about Google Drive is their cost. No other cloud storage solution can beat it's price I reckon. I'm using 2TB storage for just about $15 CAD per month. How insane is that?
cons: No complains so far. Absolutely in love with Google Drive. I just wished if they had more options to upgrade or degrade the storage size. Right now, I'm on a 2TB plan, but I'm barely using that. I wanted to degrade the size to 1TB, but they don't seem to have an option for that.

review_title: A simple tool that just works
description: Google Drive has been a consistently reliable tool for both personal and professional use. Real-time collaboration, easy sharing, and seamless access across devices make everyday work faster and more organized. It removes a lot of friction from teamwork and content creation, which is why it remains my go-to platform.
author: Elena T.
pros: The free plan is generous and genuinely useful. Sharing files, collaborating in real time, leaving comments, and tracking changes all work smoothly in a single document. It’s extremely easy to use and makes teamwork feel natural. Having everything accessible in one place for both work and personal use is a big advantage. I never used customer support, but Google is always amazing and replies very fast.
cons: As projects and shared folders grow, file organization can become cluttered and harder to manage. Permission settings are powerful but not always intuitive, especially when many collaborators are involved.

review_title: Google is the gold standard
description: Used it for 15 years for 3 jobs and entrepreneurial initiatives as well, and have never been frustrated with it
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's free. Never needed support because it just works. It's intuitive and there's not bugs. It hosts documents better than outlook. Makes it easy to share files and send them with others. It's cheap for extra storage. google search on my files? yes please
cons: I dislike that my current company uses outlook, and Maybe google can up its conversion efforts and save those of us stuck on it.

review_title: “Essential for Keeping Our Business Organized”
description: Our overall experience with Google Drive has been excellent. It’s reliable, easy to use, and has made a real difference in how efficiently we run Infinity Energy Spa & Crystal Shop. It’s an essential tool for keeping our documents, photos, and team communications organized and accessible.
author: Dawn G.
pros: What I appreciate most about Google Drive is how effortlessly it keeps our team organized and connected. The shared folders make it easy to access product photos, marketing materials, and workshop documents from anywhere—saving us hours every week. The intuitive layout means even our less tech-savvy team members can find what they need quickly. It’s become the backbone of our daily operations, making collaboration smooth and stress-free.
cons: Occasionally, organizing shared folders can get confusing if multiple people are moving files at the same time. But overall, these are minor issues compared to the benefits.

review_title: Easy to Use, Tons of Integration Options
description: It's a really pleasant platform, much preferred over using OneDrive as I used to. Everything you need to get out of it is easy to understand & utilize.
author: Katelynn F.
pros: I love the integration options it has with so many different softwares/platforms, making it even easier to share or upload files wherever needed.
cons: It seems like the 'date modified' section isn't always accurate, which can be a bit annoying because there have been times where I know for sure I uploaded an image 1+ month ago, but the date modified will show me 1 week ago, for example.

review_title: The Support of the Drive
description: Overall my experience with Google Drive has been a good one. I am able to keep my separate documents in My Drive and all of my shared work related documents in the Shared Google drive. Helping to create a clean digital workspace.
author: Lissa H.
pros: Google drive has proven to be a reliable system to use for storage. We have been able to manage a number of legal documents as well as transition all of our paper employee files to digital files to save space and effort. We are able to share specific documents and collaborate with other teams on editing new policies and sharing information.
cons: Initially it took time to know that we could trust Google Drive with our important HR documents. Prior to transitioning all of our employee files we were hesitant and afraid to lose employee information as many documents cannot be duplicated and need to remain in their original form.

review_title: Google Drive Review
description: I love using Google Drive. It's been a staple product in my workplace. It's very simple and easy to use. Very user-friendly!
author: Whitney J.
pros: I like that I can create folders and directly share those with my team members so that we can all work from the same drive. It's very easy to use and create folders for specific things. The search functionality is very nice, as long as I know a key word of what I'm looking for it pops it up every time. The product is worth the investment since it gives you ample amount of storage for all of your data. I also enjoy the new AI generated summaries on the documents in the details section. I haven't had to use their customer support yet, which is a pro as well.
cons: I don't have many cons for Google Drive. I love the product. It's a lot better than using computer storage for data because it stores everything in the cloud. The only thing I can think of is once a person no longer needs to have access to a certain document, it can be hard to remember to remove their access versus it just automatically taking them off.

review_title: Google Drive is great!
description: Google Drive is perfect for students, entrepreneurs, teams, new small businesses and anyone who needs easy access and collaboration without complicated setup. It’s not the most private or customizable platform, but for convenience and productivity, it’s hard to beat.
author: Hawa L.
pros: I’ve been using Google Drive for years and overall it’s one of the easiest storage tools out there, especially if you live inside Gmail, Docs, or Google Workspace.user friendly and free for those of us who aren't able to afford other platforms. You control who uses or sees your documents, easy to share files and free storage.
cons: your storage fills up fast and offline access can be tricky if not set up properly. Another con is Google having access to personal data.

review_title: Reliable storage with easy sharing
description: I’ve used Google Drive for personal and business purposes. It’s easy to organize files into folders and keep things separate. Sharing links works well for clients and coworkers, and it avoids sending long emails or attachments. Customer support isn’t very direct, but most issues I had, I could solve by checking FAQs or community posts. Overall, it’s dependable and simple enough that I feel comfortable using it every day.
author: Jeff A.
pros: Google Drive makes it really simple to store files and share them. I like that I can access everything from my phone or laptop, and I don’t have to worry about losing documents. Collaboration is great, multiple people can edit the same file at the same time, and I can see changes instantly.
cons: Sometimes the search pulls up older versions of documents before the current ones. Large files can take a while to upload, and the storage fills up faster than I expected, especially when working with photos or big PDFs.

review_title: Totally dependent on it! Great for professional work.
description: Overall, it’s reliable, simple to use, and extremely convenient for both personal and professional work. We love it!
author: Sara P.
pros: What I like most is how easy it makes storing, accessing, and sharing files from basically anywhere. The real-time collaboration is a huge advantage—working on the same file with others, seeing changes instantly, and leaving comments saves so much time and effort! It’s well-organized, integrates smoothly with Gmail, and keeps everything synced.
cons: What I like least is that organizing files can get messy over time because the folder structure isn’t always intuitive, and finding older files sometimes takes extra effort. Also, offline access can be limited, and syncing issues occasionally occur, especially with large folders or files.

review_title: Easy storage and file sharing
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Very accessible and easy to use, from mobile, desktop and tablet meaning that you are always connected to your important files. Grant permissions so the right people and teams can access and collaborate on projects. And even after you use the generous free storage you can easily increase your allowance to match your need.
cons: The offline mode has limited functionality so in areas with poor signal reach you can find yourself a bit stranded.

review_title: Best cloud storage service for personal files
description: Although I've been using Box and Dropbox in the past, Google Drive remains the favorite choice for all my personal files. The fact that I can access the files from any device - mobile, tablet, laptop - it's really helpful when I need to show certain files or share them with someone in real time. Also, the feature of making a file available offline is also good, as I can use that when travelling by plane for instance and I don't have internet access. Automatic saving is also a great feature that avoids loosing work.
author: Robert S.
pros: Clean interface, instant sync between local software and cloud, easy to access from anywhere and any device. Also, the fact that I can use the native GDocs or GSheet to create files directly in GDrive, that I can use for taking notes, or using the Excel functionalities. That way I won't need a third-party tool to take notes, so that I can have all in one place.
cons: The desktop sync application (Drive for desktop) is sometimes slow in syncing large files. Truly, it doesn't happen too often, but when you upload more than one large file at the same time, it struggles a bit - it's probably the security scanning that prevents the upload to move faster.

review_title: Long-time user of Google Drive.
description: I used Google Drive for personal projects for over 10 years, and used it for company project for 3 years. It is always stable and easy to use, help me lots in team collaboration.
author: Jim C.
pros: 1. Super easy to use, you can use it in corporate environment ,as well as personal usage, just applying different gmail account to login. 2. You can treat it as file server to store different kind of files, but also used it as a Saas portal to store workspace file, such as Google sheets, Google Docs.
cons: The authorization is decentralized and user-based, so sometimes I got lost in lots of 'shared with me' files.

review_title: 10/10 everyone should have one!
description: I have had a 10 out of 10 experience and have no complaints for this product/website. I feel that everyone should have a Google drive account especially when working in a job that requires collaboration on documents.
author: Victoria W.
pros: I have used Google drive during many different seasons of life. I used Google drive when I was in high school which attests to it ease of use and now as an adult when collaborating with different practitioners within the office. Google drive has been my go to ensure that my documents are saved and stored correctly and that I will always have access and manage my projects no matter which computer I am sitting at. I have not needed to use customer support since having a Google drive account which again shows the ease of use and no need for extra assistance. Google drive is a free site to use which makes it cost efficient for anyone with internet access.
cons: Since using google drive I have had no issues with the use of this site. I find it very user friendly and I love the ability to use it anywhere and collaborate with others without having to sit down at a group meeting.

review_title: Driving im
description: I believe that anybody who would give the drive application a chance would see themselves that it's more than just a simple storage solution. With all of the included functions & features, you can't go wrong when choosing drive to manage all of your documents & such!
author: Destiny C.
pros: Google drive was very easy to use and had lots of great functions that made it so much more enjoyable to use! The document management options have me the ability to personalized some of my saved files and folders so that it was easier to locate what I was looking for whenever I would utilize the search function. Overall, it's a wonderful application and you wouldn't regret giving it a chance.
cons: I didn't honestly have too many complains when speaking on the Google drive application. During my time using the app, I don't recall ever running into any bugs or having any technical malfunctions. All of the functions of the application worked seamlessly & without fail!

review_title: Efficient Cloud Storage Solution
description: my experience with Google Drive has been very good. It’s easy to use, convenient, and great for storing and sharing files. The real-time collaboration features are especially helpful, and it works smoothly with other Google apps. While organization in the “Shared with me” section could be better, it’s still a reliable and efficient tool overa
author: Cherry Mae C.
pros: What I like most about Google Drive is how easy and convenient it is to store, access, and share files from anywhere. Everything is automatically saved in the cloud, which means I don’t have to worry about losing documents or switching devices.
cons: uploading or syncing large files can be slow, particularly when internet speed is not strong. This can interrupt workflows when working with big videos or presentations.
